ItacPersonItem.Notes: Angela Nuovo, `Gian Vincenzo Pinelli’s collection of catalogues of private libraries in sixteenth century Europe`, in Gutenberg-Jahrbuch (2007), p. 129-44: `Matteo Macigni (c.1510–c.1582) belonged to the Collegio dei Filosofi e dei Medici in Padua and participated in two of the most significant academies of his day, the Accademia degli Infiammati and the Accademia degli Animosi.`
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
